drupalNodeEvent.title: Nuclear Safety and Security for Ukraine Amid US-China Rivalry

field_speakers.processed:
• Nicholas Roth (Nuclear Threat Initiative)
  • Mariana Budjeryn (Belfer Center, Harvard University)
  • Yanliang Pan (James Martin Center for Nonproliferation Studies, Middlebury Institute)
  • Lili Voytovych (Hertie School, Germany

 

Moderator: Kaiser Kuo

field_organizers.processed:
- The Center for Slavic, Eurasian, and East European Studies (CSEEES) at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill.

- Ukrainian Association of Sinologists.

- Sinica Podcast.

- A.Krymskyi Institute of Oriental Studies.

field_description.processed:
As global strategic competition heightens, Ukraine's nuclear safety is a pressing issue. This roundtable convenes experts from U.S., Europe, and China-focused platforms to delve into key questions:
◼️ Identifying Ukraine's current nuclear safety risks.
◼️ Contrasting U.S. and Chinese stances on nuclear deterrence, arms control, and security assurances within the Ukrainian context.
◼️ Assessing the IAEA's efficacy in upholding nuclear security in Ukraine amidst great-power rivalry.
